Enron's stock price falls as company faces scrutiny
By David W Delainey

10/27/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

HOUSTON, Oct 27 (Reuters) - Enron Corp., the Houston energy trader, said on Monday it would cut about $1 billion in costs and restructure its business to focus on its core energy trading business.=20
The company said it would sell its trading operations in Europe and Asia to a unit of U.S. energy giant Enron Corp. (ENE.N) and would focus on its core energy trading business.=20
Enron said it would also restructure its board of directors and cut about $1 billion in expenses.=20
The company said it would also sell its power trading business in the United States to Dynegy Inc. (DYN.N) and would focus on its core energy trading business.=20
